title: What is the best method to connect monday boards data to Power BI?
excerpt:
Hello, 
Is there a go to method to integrate Monday.com with power BI? 
I need to get the data from all the boards and import it into power BI, can someone suggest a free solution? 
Thanks

posts.author: SolidBingo (Solid Bingo)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,

Is there a go to method to integrate Monday.com with power BI?

I need to get the data from all the boards and import it into power BI, can someone suggest a free solution?

Thanks

posts.author: GiannisKoukounas (Giannis Koukounas)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @SolidBingo ,

Welcome to the monday community!

Since there is currently no native integration of monday with Power BI, it would need to be created via using platforms such as Make.com .

Our team at thespelas.com has a great deal of experience in creating complex integrations, so if you are interested in going forward with this integration please contact us at info@thespelas.com to see how we could possibly assist.

Best Regards,
Giannis, Implementation Consultant at thespelas.com

posts.author: Khrystyna_Shparyk (Khrystyna)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,

you can try to use Power BI Connector for monday.com by Alpha Serve. With this app you can connect monday.com to Power BI and build comprehensive reports in minutes for data-driven decisions.

posts.author: nicstt (Nicholas)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @SolidBingo

I’m late to the party here, but thought to post what I’ve done.

I’ve already used Tugger for a couple of their other connectors (https://tuggerapp.com/) and noticed that Monday has been added by them.

They offer a 30 day free trial and are super-helpful if you need to reach out.

It is a really simple process: sign up for a trial; add your connector; wait for our data; and then use the provided OData link and secure credentials to import into Power BI.

posts.author: YOGESHP (Yogesh Pokale)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @SolidBingo We’re not using any third-party tool to connect our monday.com boards to Power BI. Instead, we use the monday.com API along with Power Query, which allows our boards to connect directly to Power BI. We also have a scheduled refresh set up so the data updates automatically at the defined frequency.

Please let me know if this approach would be helpful for you. If so, I’d be happy to share the query. Thanks!

posts.author: ricoissuave (Rico)
posts.cookedHtml: Can you please send me your query for the life of me I cannot connect and get JSON returned.

posts.author: YOGESHP (Yogesh Pokale)
posts.cookedHtml:
@ricoissuave Welcome to the community!

Try this

let
// GraphQL query with placeholders
queryText =
“{”“query”“:”“query getItems($cursor: String) { boards(ids: BOARD_ID) { items_page(limit: 500, cursor: $cursor) { items { id name column_values(ids: [““COLUMN_ID_1””, ““COLUMN_ID_2””, ““COLUMN_ID_3””, ““COLUMN_ID_4””, ““COLUMN_ID_5””]) { id text } } cursor } } }”“}”,

// API request
Source =
Web.Contents(
“https://api.monday.com/v2”,
[
Headers = [
#“Content-Type” = “application/json”,
#“Authorization” = “Bearer YOUR_API_KEY”
],
Content = Text.ToBinary(queryText)
]
),

// Convert response to JSON
JsonResponse = Json.Document(Source),

// Navigate response
boards = JsonResponse[data][boards],
items_page = boards{0}[items_page],
items = items_page[items],

// Convert to table
ItemsTable =
Table.FromList(
items,
Splitter.SplitByNothing(),
{“Column1”}
),

// Expand item data
ExpandedItems =
Table.ExpandRecordColumn(
ItemsTable,
“Column1”,
{“id”, “name”, “column_values”}
),

// Expand column values list
ExpandedColumnValues =
Table.ExpandListColumn(
ExpandedItems,
“column_values”
),

// Expand column values details
FinalTable =
Table.ExpandRecordColumn(
ExpandedColumnValues,
“column_values”,
{“id”, “text”},
{“column_id”, “column_value”}
)

in
FinalTable
